---
title: Comparing methods to integrate MB tumours
date: 2023-05-10
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
---

## Goal of this analysis

To compare integration methods (CCA, Harmony, and fastMNN) when integrating MB tumours.


## Files and directories

### Code

See `<tumour Vladoiu software directory>/test_integ_methods.R`.

### Output

Output directory is at `<tumour Vladoiu results directory>/20230510`.

The following bash command was run to test the integration methods (the working directory was set as the output directory).

```bash
$ Rscript /CBL_scRNAseq/software/tumour/Vladoiu/test_integ_methods.R \
      --srat_rds /CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/Vladoiu/20230508/vladoiu_mb_merge.rds \
      --out_dir ./ \
      &> test_integ_methods.log
```

The results are stored in 3 subdirectories:

- `cca/` for the CCA integration figures;
- `harmony/` for the Harmony integration figures; and
- `mnn/` for the fastMNN integration figures.

The RDS files for each of the integration methods are stored directly in the output directory.


## Results

Note that `SCTransform` was run on the merged dataset before the integration methods were run (see flowchart below).

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-integ-flowchart
%%| fig-cap: Flowchart showing normalization, integration, and dimensionality reduction steps
flowchart TB
    merge["Import merged tumour samples"] --> sct["Re-run `SCTransform` on merged samples"]
    sct --> cca["CCA"] & harmony["Harmony"] & mnn["fastMNN"] --> umap["Dimensionality reduction (UMAP)"]
    umap --> clustering["Clustering and downstream visualization"]
```

::: {#fig-integ-umaps}

![(a) Seurat CCA integration](/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/Vladoiu/20230510/cca/umap.png)

![(b) Harmony integration](/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/Vladoiu/20230510/harmony/umap.png)

![(c) fastMNN integration](/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/Vladoiu/20230510/mnn/umap.png)

UMAP visualizations for each integration methods with cells coloured by sample (left), cluster number (middle), and tumour subtype (right)

:::

The integration methods seem to be relatively similar overall, with the tumours mostly clustering by subtype (@fig-integ-umaps, right panels).

Based on [pre-print](https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2021.08.04.453579v1) from Pugh lab, fastMNN is probably the best method for integrating the tumour samples.
